title: taraxacum primigenium
intro: Taraxacum primigenium, also known as wild dandelion, is a perennial plant belonging to the sunflower family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. The plant's leaves, roots, and flowers have been shown to provide several health benefits, including reducing blood pressure, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Taraxacum primigenium is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
